title: BEM-875 internal review
objective:
Create file governance/audit/internal/results/BEM875_INTERNAL_PROTOCOL_V13_REVIEW.md with content # BEM-875 INTERNAL PROTOCOL V1.3 REVIEW
Status: INTERNAL_RESULT_READY
Findings:
- Operator additions are consistent with BEM-871 curator-mediated route.
- Pre-P0 cleanup is mandatory before P0 reliability work.
- Worker must be modeled as Curator plus multiple internal contours.
- Parallel execution is acceptable only with explicit task IDs, dependency IDs, isolated workspaces, and integration lane.
- Architecture control must be a system registry, not informal notes: target model, actual model, delta, remaining work.
- Communication channel and time registry must be separated: channel moves data; registry records events and decisions.
- Director and Worker communication should use a traceable task registry as the baseline.
- Product repositories should remain separate from the control-plane, which acts as factory and architect.
Evidence:
- governance/protocols/CURATOR_MEDIATED_INTERNAL_AUDIT_PROTOCOL.md
- governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM875_PROTOCOL_V13_REQUEST.md
- Uploaded operator protocol and AI agents summary.
- Open-source practice: supervisor orchestration, handoffs, stateful workflow graphs, guardrails, tracing, typed outputs.
Agreed changes:
- Protocol v1.3 should include Pre-P0 cleanup, multi-contour Worker scaling, isolated development areas, integration lane, architecture registry, policy-governed communication, time registry, enterprise segment, ERP pilot placeholder, and product-repo factory model.
Recommendation:
- Approve v1.3 with notes and submit to operator for review.
No issue comments.
